Description: Establish team communication channels (Slack/Discord). Define roles and responsibilities. Set up regular meeting schedule.
Finalize NutriVision AI project scope: Context-Aware Nutritional Assessment using NOVA classification. Document problem statement and objectives.
Download Open Food Facts dataset (en.openfoodfacts.org.products.tsv). Verify data integrity and document dataset characteristics.
Initialize GitHub repository with proper structure. Set up .gitignore for Python projects. Create initial README.md with project overview.
Set up Python virtual environment. Install initial dependencies (pandas numpy matplotlib seaborn scikit-learn). Document setup process.
Write introduction section covering: problem statement health-washing concerns target users (health-conscious consumers nutritionists) motivation for NOVA classification.
Research and document NOVA food classification system (Group 1: Unprocessed Group 2: Processed culinary ingredients Group 3: Processed Group 4: Ultra-processed).
Create detailed project management plan with timeline task breakdown resource allocation risk assessment. Export to CSV for GitHub Projects.
Define project success metrics: model accuracy (>85% for XGBoost) reconstruction error thresholds for Autoencoder user experience metrics system response time.
Identify technical risks (model performance data drift imbalanced classes). Document mitigation strategies for each identified risk.
Finalize technology stack: Python (scikit-learn XGBoost TensorFlow/PyTorch) Frontend (React Vite Tailwind) Deployment considerations.
Design data processing pipeline: raw data cleaning / feature engineering  / model input. Create architecture diagram.
Plan model architectures: XGBoost (hyperparameters tree depth learning rate) Autoencoder (encoder/decoder layers activation functions).
Document detailed weekly milestones and deliverables through Week 7. Create Gantt chart or timeline visualization.
Load Open Food Facts dataset. Inspect data structure column types missing value patterns data distribution. Document findings.
Handle missing values in critical columns (nutrients_100g additives NOVA groups). Implement appropriate imputation strategies or removal logic.
Identify and handle outliers in nutritional data (impossible values negative nutrients >100g/100g). Document cleaning decisions.
Create engineered features: sugar-to-fiber ratio saturated-fat-to-total-fat ratio protein-quality indicators processing-level indicators.
Complete univariate analysis: distribution plots for all numerical features frequency analysis for categorical variables summary statistics.
Perform bivariate analysis: correlation analysis between nutrients relationship between additives and NOVA groups feature importance for NOVA prediction.
Analyze NOVA group distribution: class balance (check for imbalances) characteristics of each NOVA group average nutrient profiles per group.
Create comprehensive visualizations: heatmaps boxplots distribution plots 3D scatter plots (if applicable). Save publication-quality figures.
Write dataset summary section: data source description data characteristics cleaning methodology EDA findings class distribution analysis.
Export cleaned datasets: full cleaned dataset training/validation/test splits (70/15/15) whole foods subset for Autoencoder. Document data splits.
Research XGBoost algorithm: gradient boosting theory XGBoost advantages for tabular data hyperparameter tuning strategies. Document findings.
Research Autoencoder architectures: encoder-decoder structure anomaly detection applications loss functions for nutritional data. Document findings.
Research ultra-processed foods and health outcomes. Review existing NOVA classification methodologies. Identify gaps in current nutritional apps.
Implement simple baseline models: Logistic Regression Decision Tree for NOVA classification. Establish baseline performance metrics.
Implement XGBoost classifier with default hyperparameters. Create training pipeline with proper data preprocessing and cross-validation.
Design and implement initial Autoencoder architecture: define encoder/decoder layers activation functions loss function. Train on whole foods subset.
Perform feature importance analysis using XGBoost feature importance. Identify top predictive features. Test model with reduced feature sets.
Write background section covering: NOVA classification system XGBoost methodology Autoencoder theory ultra-processed foods and health existing solutions.
Set up comprehensive evaluation framework: classification metrics (accuracy precision recall F1) confusion matrices cross-validation strategy. Create reusable code.
Perform hyperparameter optimization: max_depth learning_rate n_estimators subsample colsample_bytree. Use GridSearchCV or RandomizedSearchCV.
Address class imbalance if present: SMOTE oversampling class_weight adjustment undersampling majority class. Compare strategies.
Fine-tune XGBoost based on validation performance. Implement early stopping. Optimize for production (model size inference speed).
Experiment with Autoencoder architectures: layer depths bottleneck size dropout regularization batch normalization. Track reconstruction errors.
Determine optimal reconstruction error threshold for anomaly detection. Analyze distribution of reconstruction errors for different NOVA groups.
Explore ensemble methods: combining multiple XGBoost models stacking with other classifiers weighted voting. Compare performance.
Implement model saving/loading: serialize XGBoost model save Autoencoder weights create model versioning system. Document model artifacts.
Write methods section covering: data preprocessing feature engineering XGBoost implementation and tuning Autoencoder architecture training procedures evaluation metrics.
Plan backend-frontend integration: API endpoint design request/response formats error handling model inference pipeline.
Comprehensive analysis of final models: per-class performance error analysis feature importance visualization comparative analysis with baselines.
Perform statistical tests: compare model performances test generalization on holdout set validate cross-validation results. Document findings.
Generate SHAP values for XGBoost model. Create SHAP visualizations (summary plots dependence plots). Document feature contributions.
Develop Flask/FastAPI backend: endpoint for nutritional data input XGBoost prediction endpoint Autoencoder anomaly detection endpoint. Include error handling.
Enhance Dashboard component: display NOVA classification show reconstruction error/anomaly score visualize nutritional profile personalization inputs.
Create visualization components: NOVA group indicator nutritional breakdown chart additive flagging personalized recommendations display.
Implement user profile system: dietary restrictions (halal vegan gluten-free) health conditions micronutrient goals. Create rule-based recommendation engine.
Test full pipeline: frontend input → API call → model prediction → response display. Test edge cases error handling.
Write initial results section: model performance metrics confusion matrices comparative analysis with baselines. Include tables and figures.
Generate publication-quality figures: confusion matrices ROC curves precision-recall curves learning curves feature importance plots SHAP visualizations.
Complete results section: qualitative analysis limitations discussion real-world application examples system performance metrics.
Write discussion section: interpretation of results comparison with existing solutions implications for nutrition tracking limitations and constraints.
Document future work: OCR integration for label scanning real-time data drift monitoring recipe reformulation tracking deployment to mobile platform.
Write conclusion summarizing: project achievements model performance practical applications contribution to nutritional AI field.
Write abstract (1-2 paragraphs): problem introduction (1-2 sentences) methods description (3-4 sentences) results summary (1-2 sentences).
Compile all references. Format citations properly (APA/IEEE). Verify all in-text citations match reference list.
Format report to template requirements. Proofread entire document. Check page limits (15 pages for team of 2). Insert figures/tables.
Clean GitHub repository: remove scratch code add comprehensive comments update README with usage instructions document all code files.
Create comprehensive repository documentation: README with project overview setup instructions usage examples requirements.txt update docstrings for all functions.
Export all Jupyter notebooks to PDF: data cleaning notebook EDA notebook model training notebooks analysis notebooks.
Create PowerPoint presentation (minimum 5 visualizations for team of 2): title slide problem statement data & methods results & evaluation demo/application conclusion.
Write presentation script ensuring: each member speaks 2+ minutes technical depth appropriate for CTO/VP audience time limit 5-10 minutes smooth transitions.
Rehearse presentation multiple times: time presentation (5-10 min target) refine transitions practice demo ensure equal speaking time.
Record final presentation video: high quality audio/video each member speaks 2+ minutes demonstrate system upload to YouTube/Vimeo.
Submit final report PDF (15 pages max for team of 2). Verify all sections complete formatting correct all deliverables included.
Submit final GitHub repository: verify public access or instructor collaboration share repository URL verify all code present documentation complete.
Submit presentation video URL (YouTube/Vimeo). Verify video accessibility length within limits (5-10 min) audio/video quality.
Complete peer evaluation form individually. Provide constructive feedback for teammate. Submit confidential evaluation.
Conduct comprehensive end-to-end testing: test all user workflows verify model predictions accuracy test error handling cross-browser compatibility (if web app).
Create supplementary demo video showing: system walkthrough example predictions user interaction personalization features. Upload separately from presentation.
